description: Entity Schema Designer runs in two steps: (1) call Database Research Agent with terminateAfter: false, then (2) design the schema and present it with taskComplete: true + nextStep.type: Chat + responseForm for approval.

example_1_step1_call_db_research.description: Step 1: Research FK targets before designing. ALWAYS terminateAfter: false so Schema Designer continues after research returns.

subAgent.name: Database Research Agent
subAgent.message: Are there entities named 'Projects' or 'Users' or similar in __mj_UDT or __mj? Give me the exact schema and table names.

example_2_step2_present_schema.description: Step 2: After research returns — design schema and present for approval. nextStep.type: Chat is REQUIRED to deliver message and responseForm to the user. Without it the agent terminates silently.
example_2_step2_present_schema.message:
Here's the proposed table for **Project Milestones**:

| Column           | Type            | Required | Description                              |
|------------------|-----------------|----------|------------------------------------------|
| Title            | string(200)     | Yes      | Milestone label                          |
| Status           | string(50)      | Yes      | Active / Completed / Cancelled           |
| DueDate          | datetime        | No       | Target completion date                   |
| CompletedDate    | datetime        | No       | Actual completion date                   |
| Notes            | text            | No       | Free-text description                    |
| ProjectID        | uuid (FK)       | Yes      | → Projects.ID (soft — not yet confirmed) |
| AssignedUserID   | uuid (FK)       | No       | → __mj.User.ID                           |

Auto-managed: `ID`, `__mj_CreatedAt`, `__mj_UpdatedAt`
Schema: `__mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones`

SchemaDesign.Prototype:
| Column           | Type            | Required | Description                              |
|------------------|-----------------|----------|------------------------------------------|
| Title            | string(200)     | Yes      | Milestone label                          |
| Status           | string(50)      | Yes      | Active / Completed / Cancelled           |
| DueDate          | datetime        | No       | Target completion date                   |
| CompletedDate    | datetime        | No       | Actual completion date                   |
| Notes            | text            | No       | Free-text description                    |
| ProjectID        | uuid (FK)       | Yes      | → Projects.ID (soft — not yet confirmed) |
| AssignedUserID   | uuid (FK)       | No       | → __mj.User.ID                           |

TableDefinition.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects

Columns.Name: CompletedDate
Columns.Description: Actual date the milestone was completed

Columns.Name: ProjectID
Columns.Description: FK → Projects.ID. Research found no Projects table in __mj_UDT — using soft FK.

example_3_alter_add_column.description: Adding a new column to an existing entity — same two-step pattern. nextStep.type: Chat is always required.
example_3_alter_add_column.message:
Updated — here's what was added to **Project Milestones**:

| Column   | Type    | Required | Description               |
|----------|---------|----------|---------------------------|
| Priority | integer | No       | 1 (high) – 5 (low) scale  |

Auto-managed: `ID`, `__mj_CreatedAt`, `__mj_UpdatedAt`
Schema: `__mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones`

SchemaDesign.Prototype:
**Added columns:**

|----------|---------|----------|---------------------------|
| Priority | integer | No       | 1 (high) – 5 (low) scale |

TableDefinition.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects
